questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What is the only sign shape used exclusively for a stop sign?
questions.explanation: The octagon shape is reserved for stop signs so drivers can recognize them even when the lettering is obscured.

questions.prompt: A steady red traffic light means you must:
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires a full stop, and you may not proceed until the signal turns green and the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing red traffic signal mean?
choices: Treat it the same as a stop sign
The signal is broken, so proceed without stopping
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A flashing red light means come to a complete stop, then proceed when it is safe, just like at a stop sign.

questions.prompt: A yellow diamond-shaped sign is used to:
choices: Warn of conditions or hazards ahead
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs are warning signs that alert drivers to hazards such as curves, intersections, or merges ahead.

questions.prompt: What color are signs that mark construction and work zones?
questions.explanation: Orange signs and markings indicate construction or maintenance work zones where extra caution is required.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the centerline means:
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited because it is unsafe to cross into oncoming traffic there.

questions.prompt: A steady yellow traffic light means:
choices: The light is about to turn red, so prepare to stop
Speed up to beat the red light
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that red is coming, so you should stop safely if you can rather than speed up.

questions.prompt: What does a white painted line between lanes indicate?
choices: Traffic is traveling in the same direction
Traffic is traveling in opposite directions
The road is a one-way street ending
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic moving in the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: A downward-pointing triangle sign means:
questions.explanation: The inverted triangle is the yield sign, telling you to slow down and give the right-of-way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: A green traffic signal means:
choices: Proceed if the intersection is clear and it is safe
questions.explanation: A green light allows you to go, but only after making sure the intersection is clear of vehicles and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does a pennant-shaped (sideways triangle) sign warn of?
questions.explanation: The pennant-shaped sign is posted on the left side of the road to mark the beginning of a no-passing zone.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, if two vehicles arrive at the same time, who goes first?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop together, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When making a left turn at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: Oncoming traffic and pedestrians in the crosswalk
No one if you have a green light
questions.explanation: A driver turning left must give the right-of-way to oncoming traffic and to pedestrians crossing the road.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren, you should:
choices: Pull over to the right and stop
Speed up to get out of the way
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: When entering a roadway from a private driveway, you must:
choices: Yield to all traffic and pedestrians on the road
Proceed because driveways have the right-of-way
Stop only if a sign is posted
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering from a driveway or alley must yield to everyone already using the roadway.

questions.prompt: Vehicles already in a roundabout have the right-of-way over:
questions.explanation: Drivers entering a roundabout must yield to traffic already circulating inside it.

questions.prompt: How far in advance should you signal a turn or lane change?
choices: At least 100 feet before the turn
Just as you begin to turn
Only if other cars are nearby
At least 500 feet before the turn
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet ahead gives other drivers and pedestrians time to react to your intentions.

questions.prompt: At an intersection with no signs or signals, you should:
choices: Yield to vehicles that arrived first and to those on your right
Stop completely and wait one minute
questions.explanation: At an uncontrolled intersection, yield to vehicles already there and, if you arrive together, to the driver on your right.

questions.prompt: A pedestrian is crossing in a marked crosswalk. You should:
choices: Stop and yield to the pedestrian
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield the right-of-way to pedestrians within marked and unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: When making a right turn, you should generally turn from and into:
choices: The lane nearest the right curb
questions.explanation: Right turns should be made from the rightmost lane into the lane nearest the right curb to keep traffic predictable.

questions.prompt: Before turning, in addition to signaling, you should:
choices: Check mirrors and blind spots for other road users
questions.explanation: Checking mirrors and blind spots helps you spot cyclists, pedestrians, and vehicles before you turn.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in a Pennsylvania residential or business district is:
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania's default speed limit in residential and business districts is 25 mph unless signs say otherwise.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit on a rural Pennsylvania road is:
questions.explanation: The default speed limit on rural Pennsylvania roads and most highways is 55 mph unless a different limit is posted.

questions.prompt: What is a safe minimum following distance under good conditions?
choices: Four seconds behind the vehicle ahead
questions.explanation: A four-second following distance gives you time to react and stop safely if the vehicle ahead slows suddenly.

questions.prompt: In rain, snow, or heavy traffic, you should:
choices: Reduce speed and increase following distance
Maintain the posted speed limit exactly
Increase speed to clear conditions quickly
questions.explanation: Slowing down and leaving more space gives you extra time to react when traction and visibility are reduced.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in an urban district is:
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania's default urban district speed limit is 35 mph unless signs indicate a different limit.

questions.prompt: As your speed doubles, your stopping distance:
questions.explanation: Braking distance grows faster than speed, so doubling your speed more than doubles the distance needed to stop.

questions.prompt: When should you increase your following distance beyond four seconds?
choices: At night, in bad weather, or when being tailgated
Never, four seconds is always enough
questions.explanation: Poor visibility, slick roads, and tailgaters all reduce your margin for error, so you need more space.

questions.prompt: Driving too fast for conditions means:
choices: Driving at an unsafe speed even if it is under the posted limit
questions.explanation: You must adjust your speed to conditions, which can be unsafe even when you are below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: Before entering a sharp curve, you should:
choices: Brake hard in the middle of the curve
questions.explanation: Reducing speed before the curve helps you maintain control, since braking hard while turning can cause a skid.

questions.prompt: When following a large truck or motorcycle, you should:
choices: Match their exact speed bumper to bumper
questions.explanation: Extra space gives you more time to react because trucks block your view and motorcycles can stop quickly.

questions.prompt: When may you legally pass another vehicle?
choices: When a broken yellow line is on your side and it is safe
On a hill where you cannot see ahead
When a school bus has red lights flashing
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line on your side allows passing when you have a clear view and enough space to do so safely.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, the left lane is intended for:
questions.explanation: The left lane should be kept clear for passing, so slower traffic should stay in the right lanes.

questions.prompt: When a school bus stops with red lights flashing and stop arm extended, you must:
choices: Stop at least 10 feet away in both directions
Stop only if children are visible
questions.explanation: All traffic in both directions must stop at least 10 feet from a school bus with flashing red lights and an extended stop arm.

questions.prompt: Before returning to your lane after passing, you should:
choices: See the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
questions.explanation: Returning only when you can see the whole vehicle in your mirror ensures you have left enough room.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should check your blind spot by:
questions.explanation: Mirrors do not show the blind spot, so a quick shoulder glance is needed to spot vehicles beside you.

questions.prompt: You should not pass within how many feet of an intersection or railroad crossing?
questions.explanation: Passing within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad crossing is unsafe and prohibited.

questions.prompt: On a multilane road, the general rule is to:
choices: Always drive in the left lane
questions.explanation: Keeping right except when passing keeps faster traffic flowing and reduces conflicts.

questions.prompt: You may NOT pass a school bus stopped with flashing red lights when:
choices: You are on the same roadway with no separating median
There is a divided highway with a barrier between directions
You are on a separate roadway
questions.explanation: You must stop unless a clearly defined median or barrier separates your lanes from the bus.

questions.prompt: Passing on the right is generally:
choices: Allowed only in limited, specific situations
questions.explanation: Passing on the right is permitted only in certain cases, such as multilane roads, and should be done with caution.

questions.prompt: When you want to pass, you should pass only when:
choices: You have a clear view of the road ahead and enough space
Traffic is heavy in both directions
questions.explanation: Passing safely requires a clear view far enough ahead and a gap large enough to complete the maneuver.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicycle in Pennsylvania, you must give it at least:
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania law requires drivers to give bicycles at least four feet of space when passing.

questions.prompt: A motorcycle approaching an intersection should be treated as:
choices: A full-size vehicle entitled to its own lane
Something you can share a lane with
questions.explanation: Motorcycles have the same rights to a full lane as any other vehicle and should never be crowded.

questions.prompt: The large blind spots around trucks and buses are called:
questions.explanation: No-Zones are the areas around a large truck where the driver cannot see other vehicles.

questions.prompt: Pennsylvania's Move Over Law requires you to move over or:
choices: Slow to at least 20 mph below the posted speed limit
questions.explanation: If you cannot move over for a stopped emergency or disabled vehicle, you must slow to at least 20 mph under the limit.

questions.prompt: If you cannot see a truck driver's mirrors, it means:
choices: You are in a safe spot
You should speed up next to it
questions.explanation: If the truck's mirrors are not visible to you, you are in a blind spot where the driver cannot see your vehicle.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with pedestrians, drivers should:
choices: Yield and watch carefully at crosswalks
questions.explanation: Pedestrians are vulnerable, so drivers must yield and stay alert, especially at crosswalks and intersections.

questions.prompt: When following a motorcycle, you should:
choices: Follow closely since they are small
questions.explanation: Motorcycles can stop quickly and are harder to see, so extra following distance helps prevent a crash.

questions.prompt: When you encounter a horse-drawn vehicle or animal on the road, you should:
choices: Slow down and pass with care
questions.explanation: Animals can be startled, so slow down and give plenty of room when passing horse-drawn vehicles or livestock.

questions.prompt: Bicyclists on the road have:
choices: The same rights and responsibilities as drivers
No right to use the road
The right to ignore traffic signals
questions.explanation: Cyclists are considered legitimate road users with the same rights and duties as motor vehicle operators.

questions.prompt: When a tow truck with flashing lights is stopped on the shoulder, the Move Over Law requires you to:
choices: Move into a farther lane or slow down
questions.explanation: The Move Over Law covers tow trucks and disabled vehicles with warning lights, not just emergency vehicles.

questions.prompt: For drivers 21 and older in Pennsylvania, the legal BAC limit is:
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are considered legally impaired at a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08 percent.

questions.prompt: Under Pennsylvania's zero-tolerance law, drivers under 21 can be charged with DUI at a BAC of:
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 face a strict 0.02 percent limit, so even a small amount of alcohol can result in a DUI.

questions.prompt: The only thing that can sober up a person who has been drinking is:
questions.explanation: Only time allows the body to remove alcohol; coffee and other tricks do not speed up the process.

questions.prompt: Under Pennsylvania's Paul Miller's Law, it is a primary offense to:
choices: Use a handheld mobile device while driving
questions.explanation: Paul Miller's Law bans handheld phone use while driving and police can stop you for it as a primary offense.

questions.prompt: Which of the following can impair your ability to drive safely?
choices: Alcohol, illegal drugs, and some prescription medications
questions.explanation: Many substances, including legal medications, can slow reactions and impair judgment behind the wheel.

questions.prompt: The safest way to avoid driving impaired is to:
choices: Plan a sober ride before drinking
questions.explanation: Arranging a designated driver or other sober transportation before drinking prevents impaired driving.

questions.prompt: Texting while driving is dangerous mainly because it:
choices: Takes your eyes, hands, and attention off driving
questions.explanation: Texting combines visual, manual, and mental distraction, making it one of the most dangerous behaviors while driving.

questions.prompt: Alcohol affects driving by:
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reflexes, reduces coordination, and impairs the judgment needed to drive safely.

questions.prompt: If you refuse a chemical test for alcohol when lawfully arrested, Pennsylvania law typically results in:
choices: A license suspension under implied consent
questions.explanation: Under Pennsylvania's implied consent law, refusing a chemical test leads to an automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: To reduce distractions while driving, you should:
choices: Set up navigation and music before you start driving
Eat a full meal while driving
questions.explanation: Handling navigation, music, and other tasks before you drive keeps your attention on the road.

questions.prompt: In Pennsylvania, you must turn on your headlights whenever:
choices: Your windshield wipers are in continuous use
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania law requires headlights on whenever wipers are running continuously due to rain, snow, or fog.

questions.prompt: In fog, you should use:
questions.explanation: Low beams reduce glare in fog, while high beams reflect off the fog and make it harder to see.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Turn the wheel sharply opposite the skid
questions.explanation: Easing off the accelerator and steering gently where you want the front to go helps you regain control.

questions.prompt: Which part of the road is most likely to freeze first?
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses freeze before other roads because cold air circulates above and below them.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning occurs when:
choices: Tires ride on a film of water and lose road contact
questions.explanation: At higher speeds in rain, tires can lose grip by riding on water, causing a loss of steering and braking control.

questions.prompt: If your car starts to hydroplane, you should:
choices: Ease off the accelerator and keep the wheel straight
questions.explanation: Gently lifting off the gas and keeping the wheel straight lets the tires regain contact with the road.

questions.prompt: To reduce the risk of hydroplaning, you should:
choices: Slow down and keep tires properly inflated
questions.explanation: Lower speeds and good tire tread and pressure help tires channel water and keep their grip.

questions.prompt: When driving in heavy snow or ice, you should:
choices: Reduce speed and increase following distance
questions.explanation: Slick surfaces lengthen stopping distance, so slowing down and leaving extra space is essential.

questions.prompt: When sun glare makes it hard to see, you should:
choices: Use your sun visor and slow down
Speed up to get past it
questions.explanation: Lowering the visor and reducing speed helps you keep control when glare limits your vision.

questions.prompt: If you are driving and a heavy downpour reduces visibility to almost nothing, you should:
choices: Pull off the road safely and wait if needed
Speed up to get through it
questions.explanation: If you cannot see safely, pulling completely off the roadway and waiting is safer than continuing to drive.

questions.prompt: You may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
questions.explanation: Parking within 15 feet of a fire hydrant is prohibited so firefighters can access it in an emergency.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Turning wheels toward the curb when facing downhill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Facing uphill, turning the wheels away from the curb lets the tire roll back against the curb if the car moves.

questions.prompt: When parking on any hill, you should always:
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake helps prevent the vehicle from rolling regardless of the slope.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to park:
choices: On a crosswalk or in an intersection
questions.explanation: Parking on crosswalks and in intersections blocks pedestrians and traffic and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: When parking on a road with no curb, you should turn your wheels:
choices: Toward the edge of the road
Toward the center of the road
questions.explanation: Without a curb, turning the wheels toward the road's edge keeps a rolling car away from traffic.

questions.prompt: You should not park in front of:
choices: A bus you are waiting for
questions.explanation: Parking in front of a driveway blocks access and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: Before opening your door into traffic after parking, you should:
choices: Check for approaching vehicles and bicycles
questions.explanation: Checking traffic before opening your door prevents collisions with passing cars and cyclists.

questions.prompt: A curb painted to indicate no parking, or a sign saying so, means you must:
choices: Not park there at any time stated
questions.explanation: Posted no-parking signs and curb markings must be obeyed to keep traffic and access clear.

questions.prompt: When you leave a parked position at the curb, you must:
choices: Signal and yield to passing traffic
Expect traffic to stop for you
questions.explanation: Drivers leaving a parking space must signal and yield to vehicles already moving in the lane.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a learner's permit in Pennsylvania?
questions.explanation: In Pennsylvania you may apply for a learner's permit at age 16 after passing the required tests.

questions.prompt: How many hours of adult-supervised practice must a permit holder under 18 complete before the road test?
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania requires 65 hours of supervised driving, including 10 hours at night and 5 in poor weather.

questions.prompt: Of the required supervised practice hours, how many must be at night?
questions.explanation: At least 10 of the 65 supervised practice hours must be completed during nighttime driving.

questions.prompt: A junior (under-18) license holder generally may not drive between:
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania's junior license curfew prohibits driving from 11 p.m. to 5 a.m. except for work, volunteer, or emergency reasons.

questions.prompt: When driving with a learner's permit, you must be accompanied by a licensed driver who is at least:
choices: 21 years old, seated in the front seat
questions.explanation: A permit holder must have a licensed driver at least 21 (or a spouse/parent 18+) in the front seat.

questions.prompt: For the first six months with a junior license, the driver may carry no more than:
choices: One passenger under 18 who is not immediate family
questions.explanation: During the first six months, junior drivers are limited to one non-family passenger under 18 unless a parent or guardian is present.

questions.prompt: How long must a learner's permit be held before taking the road test?
questions.explanation: Permit holders must hold the permit for at least six months of skill-building before the road test.

questions.prompt: To get a learner's permit, you must pass a knowledge test and also:
questions.explanation: Applicants must pass a vision screening and provide a physical exam form in addition to the knowledge test.

questions.prompt: Pennsylvania's licensing system for new drivers is called:
questions.explanation: The GDL system phases in driving privileges through the permit, junior license, and full license stages.

questions.prompt: The Pennsylvania knowledge (permit) test passing requirement is:
choices: Answer at least 15 of 18 questions correctly
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania's knowledge test has 18 questions and you must answer at least 15 correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: Pennsylvania tracks unsafe driving using a:
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania assigns points for moving violations, and accumulating points can lead to penalties.

questions.prompt: Accumulating how many points on your record triggers consequences such as an exam or suspension?
questions.explanation: Reaching 6 or more points results in actions like a special written exam or possible suspension.

questions.prompt: Pennsylvania's seat belt law is a primary offense for:
questions.explanation: Police may stop a vehicle solely because an occupant under 18 is not buckled, in any seating position.

questions.prompt: Children under age 4 in Pennsylvania must ride:
choices: In an approved child safety seat
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania law requires children under 4 to be secured in an approved child safety seat.

questions.prompt: A conviction for DUI in Pennsylvania can result in:
choices: License suspension, fines, and possible jail time
questions.explanation: DUI is a serious offense carrying suspension, fines, treatment requirements, and potential jail.

questions.prompt: Children under age 2 in Pennsylvania must ride in:
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania law requires children under 2 to ride in a rear-facing car seat for their safety.

questions.prompt: If a police officer signals you to pull over, you should:
choices: Pull over safely to the right and stop
Speed up to find a station
questions.explanation: Pull to the right as soon as it is safe, stop, and wait with your hands visible for the officer.

questions.prompt: For a driver under 18, accumulating 6 or more points can result in:
questions.explanation: A driver under 18 who reaches 6 or more points faces a 90-day suspension among other consequences.

questions.prompt: Driving without a valid license or while suspended in Pennsylvania can lead to:
questions.explanation: Driving while suspended or unlicensed is illegal and brings fines and further penalties.

questions.prompt: Points on a Pennsylvania driving record are reduced by:
questions.explanation: Points are removed gradually as you continue to drive without committing further violations.

questions.prompt: All drivers and front-seat passengers in Pennsylvania are required to:
choices: Wear seat belts only on highways
Wear seat belts only at night
Wear seat belts only in winter
questions.explanation: Pennsylvania law requires drivers and front-seat passengers to wear seat belts at all times.
